seniority.level: Staff / Principal IC or Senior PM Lead
level_signals: 10+ years of experience in high-scale, high-reliability developer-facing software
Comp range $305K-$385K base, consistent with Staff/Principal IC at Anthropic
Owned a revenue number, margin target, or channel P&L — commercial ownership signal

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have 10+ years of experience in high-scale, high-reliability developer-facing software.
Have 5+ years of product management experience.
Have built or shipped products with a hyperscaler as a partner — you know how to align goals and deliver impact across that boundary.
Have owned a revenue number, a margin target, or a channel P&L — not just a product roadmap.
Operate with high autonomy in ambiguous, fast-moving environments.
Are an excellent written communicator and able to drive clarity out of complexity.
Excel at cross-company stakeholder management and can build trusted relationships at every layer of an organization.
Minimum education: Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience as a founder, GM, or early-stage operator where you owned commercial outcomes, not just product outputs.
Direct experience as a PM at AWS, GCP, or Azure — or at an ISV that ships through one of their marketplaces.

scope.surface: Enterprise API and multi-cloud integrations on the Anthropic Platform (enterprise fabric area — authentication, rate limiting, compliance, SDKs, integration frameworks)
scope.business_model: B2B / enterprise API platform with hyperscaler (Google Cloud) channel partnership
primary_metrics_inferred: Channel revenue / P&L through hyperscaler marketplace
Enterprise API adoption and integration success
core_responsibilities: Deeply engage with enterprise developers and technical leaders to understand their integration needs and pain points
Run regular feedback sessions and technical reviews with key enterprise customers
Build strong relationships with enterprise development teams to understand their workflows and challenges
Transform customer insights into actionable product requirements and priorities
Define and execute the enterprise API strategy, balancing security requirements with developer experience
Develop a clear roadmap for enterprise API features including authentication, rate limiting, and compliance capabilities
Identify and prioritize key enterprise integration patterns that drive organizational value
Partner with engineering to build enterprise-grade API features, security controls, and deployment tools
Design and implement enterprise integration frameworks and SDKs for common enterprise systems
Drive development of industry-specific API features and compliance capabilities
Partner with sales and customer success to understand enterprise requirements and support technical evaluations
Work closely with security and compliance teams to meet enterprise standards
Collaborate with platform teams on API architecture and scalability
Engage with marketing to develop enterprise API positioning and technical materials

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed and highly competitive ($305K-$385K base)
Visa sponsorship explicitly offered with good-faith effort commitment
Clear product surface and ownership area within Platform Org (enterprise fabric)
Encourages applications even from candidates who don't meet every qualification
Role has commercial ownership scope (revenue/P&L) — high-impact, high-visibility position
Explicit cross-functional scope gives strong signal about expected seniority and influence
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least 25% office time in San Francisco — eliminates fully-remote candidates
10+ years developer-facing software AND 5+ years PM experience is a high bar — requires a relatively senior candidate
Hyperscaler partner experience is a hard requirement that may be difficult to substitute
Comp range is broad ($80K spread), suggesting internal grade ambiguity worth clarifying in interview process

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range and 10+ year requirement)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities and 'owned a revenue number' requirement; not stated in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from Platform Org description and hyperscaler partnership requirement)
location.cities_listed (JD mentions San Francisco headquarters; no other cities named for this role)
ambiguities: Role title says 'Multi-Cloud Growth - Google' implying Google Cloud as primary hyperscaler partner, but JD body does not name Google specifically — treated as Google Cloud-focused based on title.
JD does not specify whether this is an IC or people-manager role; 'manages_people' set to false as no direct report responsibility is mentioned, but seniority level could imply future team-building.
Compensation range description notes it shows OTE for sales roles; this appears to be a PM (not sales) role — interpreted as base salary range only, not OTE.
